title: The Power of Music on Wellbeing
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Music is known to have (0) therapeutic effects on the mind and body. Listening to certain types of music can produce highly (17) beneficial changes in mood and emotional state. Studies confirm that music triggers strong (18) emotional responses, from joy and excitement to sadness and calm. Playing a musical instrument has also been shown to cause a (19) reduction in cortisol, the hormone linked to stress. Composers and musicians say that making music provides them with an endless source of (20) inspiration and purpose. Research has shown that music can produce (21) significant improvements in the memory and cognitive function of elderly patients. Many therapists now use music as a means of (22) communication with patients who cannot express their feelings in words. Learning to play an instrument requires practice, but the personal (23) improvement it brings is often remarkable. Perhaps most importantly, music creates truly (24) unforgettable experiences that stay with people throughout their lives.

example.text: Music is known to have (0) THERAPEUTIC effects on the mind and body. (THERAPY)

questions.explanation: An adjective is needed before 'changes'; 'benefit' becomes 'beneficial' by adding the suffix '-ial'.
An adjective is needed before 'responses'; 'emotion' becomes 'emotional' by adding the suffix '-al'.
A noun is needed after 'a'; 'reduce' becomes 'reduction' by replacing '-duce' with '-duction'.
A noun is needed after 'source of'; 'inspire' becomes 'inspiration' by replacing '-re' with '-ration'.
An adjective is needed before 'improvements'; 'signify' becomes 'significant' by changing '-ify' to '-icant'.
A noun is needed after 'means of'; 'communicate' becomes 'communication' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
A noun is needed after 'the personal'; 'improve' becomes 'improvement' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
A negative adjective is needed; 'forget' → 'forgettable', then the prefix 'un-' creates 'unforgettable', meaning 'impossible to forget'.
